dinka: Mony acï weu kith thiai wei.
english: The man has squandered a lot of money.
Will you be able to do it?

dinka: Cit niin bï a thiaar wei?
english: Will he not be absent for a long time?
I came when the person was absent.

dinka: Cït niin bï a thiaar wei? Will he not be absent for a long time? Ca niin?
english: Did you sleep? (are you well), good morning!
I want a job from you.

dinka: Bɛ̈n y acaa tek wɛ̈t kuny.

english: The boy is releasing the goats.

dinka: Bɛ̈n y acaa tɛk wɛt luny.
guɔ̈p riɔ̈c yen Wä nhom nhial.
english: Those who declare publicly that they belong to me, I will do the same for them before my Father in heaven.

dinka: gam lɔn cï ɣɛn gam ayic.
english: Come and stay in my house if you have decided that I am a true believer in the Lord.
It is an order of the chief.
Give me at least a tarifa.
Such a thing made me wonder.

dinka: Liemdu acït liem agany ala thok rou.
english: Your tongue is like that of the big lizard which is double (you are ambiguous).

dinka: Ku wën cï yen puɔ̈u riääk apɛi, ke cɔl amac ɣet tɛ̈ bï yen käny cool ëbën.
english: The king was very angry, and he sent the servant to jail to be punished until he should pay back the whole amount.

dinka: Kuny anyaar e puɔu kɛc apɛi.
english: The buffalo bull has very bitter heart.
It was eaten by a ferocious animal.
A bushbuck has barked last night.

dinka: Tiom rel thɛɛr apath, rap abï pur thïn ku abou.
english: Soil of old anthills is good, durra will be planted on them with maize.
He held me at the arm.

dinka: Raan ca lat ca kɔk nyin?
english: Did you ask pardon to the person you insulted?
What salary will you pay me?

dinka: Thiëc kɔc baai lɔn tɔ̈ bɛ̈n y thïn.
english: Ask the people of the village if there is the chief.
An adventurer will not grow old.
The river (fishing) is forbidden today to the people.
Don't enter my room without permission.
Are you well? (Lit: Did you rise?

dinka: Cït niin bï a thiaar wei? Will he not be absent for a long time? Acï ciɛ̈t kë war.
english: It is as it was before.

dinka: Lɔl acï nhom dhiac Kir ic.
english: The Lol empties into the Bahr el Arab.
The sick person has improved. adj. better, older, bigger.
The water is up to the knees. Pek! Enough!
The child has begun to reason.

dinka: Köör acï peu nhial but yic.
english: A lion startled up in a bush.

dinka: Rek mior karou bïk arabia la ɣoc.
english: Yoke two bulls to pull the cart.

dinka: Acï tut karou rek many tök.
english: He got two geese with one shot.
The child is very sick. Atuaany. He is getting ill.

dinka: ] Na tueny deŋ, ke röör aabï pur.
english: If it rains, then men will cultivate.

dinka: Acï thou rin tetok cï yök tɔŋ ic.
english: He died from a wound he got in a fight.
Until it is finished. SWj: agut.

dinka: Wek alɛ̈k yic, na cäk röt war bäk ciët mïth kor, ke wek aacïï bääny Nhialic bï kaŋ yök.
english: I assure you that unless you change and become like children, you will never enter the Kingdom of heaven.
The Jur river is becoming lower.

dinka: Riɛny thii acï lɔ lip tooc.
english: The boy went to the swamps (in dry season) to become fat.
You have become slender this year.

dinka: Ɣɛn acï nyiande luony kɔu weŋ.
english: I got engaged to his daughter paying part of the marriage price.
The poor man is begging from the deaf man.

dinka: Ɣöt aye gɔl e yɛp kɔɔu roor ic.
english: The house is started by cutting poles in the forest.

dinka: Ruon tui, ɣɛn abï baai yam.
english: Next year, I shall start a new home.

dinka: Tɛ̈ɛ n̈ nyin yic ke röör karou cï ruk alɛ̈t h ɣer bɔ̈ ku kɛ̈ɛ c̈ kë atuuc lɔ̈m.
english: Two men dressed in white suddenly stood beside them.
Am I giving birth to it?
The woman has pangs of birth.
He was bleeding from the nose.
I am bleeding from the nose.

dinka: Tiëŋkë Raan cï lɔc ku dɔc kïn.
Panda aröm akeuic ke pan Nuer.
english: Our country borders on the Nuer country.
She holds her child to her bosom.
The bracelets penetrated into the flesh of my arm.
Seperate the bran from the flour.

dinka: Këër tim nyuc yïn thïn abï dhoŋ.
english: The branch on which you sit will break.

dinka: Yïn aba riɔp buɔt kabɛt ë pɛɛi.
english: I shall pay you 8 pounds per month.

dinka: Acï bɛ̈n y riɔp wɛŋ tök.
english: He bribed the chief with one cow.
Don't bump yourself at the entrance.
The man held his belly for laughing.
Take the goats home. 2) take (no object).
Carry my bag. Ee manh muk panda. It is a child brought up in our home.
He was excoriated at the head.

dinka: Acï joŋde roc bï cï ̚ kat ror.
english: He castrated his dog so that it should not go astray.
Hold my fellow who got a sprain.
The spirit took possession of the man.
It has ceased to rain. (The rainy season is over.) n. stopping.
A dugout is hollowed out with a gouge.

dinka: Mony acï kuɛny bei bï ya bɛ̈ny.
english: The man has been choose to be chief.
The child is choosing a stick.
The claw of a lion is very strong.
The woman has left the hut. (A woman in confinement left the hut 3 or 4 days after child’s birth, passing over a small fire put at the entrance.) vhlp. come.

dinka: [ Gram: contracted from bï+kek] Bïk bɛn?
Ɣɛn awïc dɛ̈t thöŋ ke yen.
english: I want another one like this.

dinka: Bɛ̈ny acï kɔc pielic luoi gek.
english: The chief compelled the people to work on the road.
Don't compel the people to work.

dinka: Yɔŋ kɔc bï luoi lac thok.
english: Hurry them to finish the work quickly.
The man is a good composer of songs.

dinka: Bɛ̈ny bith acï köör theeth ë tɛ̈n.
english: The witchdoctor conjured the lion to come here (to be killed).
I shall give you a cow for consolation.

dinka: Të tɔ̈ wɛ̈ɛ ẗ thel jɔk tiim nhïïm theei, ke ɣook aapur rap.
english: When Orion in the evening is over the trees, we sow durra (in May).
He has spent all the money.
A    disease exterminated all my cattle.
The women have cooked the meat.

dinka: Meth acï läc ë ɣɔ̈k kueen.
english: The child has counted the cattle pegs.
The child is counting the tethering pegs. Kuen ̚ weuku apath. Count the money well.
Read the letter to the chief.
He was counted amongest your people.

dinka: Duk ajiep lok yic këth weŋ.
english: Don't rinse the milkgourd with cow urine.

dinka: Na ci piny bak Garang aci diit ku Abuk thiin aci guɔ diit, bik jal thiak, he shaped them with clay, put them in a pot and covered it.
english: When he uncovered it, they had grown up. At dawn Garang was full grown and the breast of Abuk was already large, so they married. NWr: aten cak e piny.
I have a debt with a person.
The field yielded a good durra crop.
This is the end of my doing! (Never more!

dinka: Yath ee jɔŋ tit dhienh ë Jaŋ ku atɔ̈ lai gup ku kä kɔ̈k yic, ku acïï Nhialic yiek kɔc war tek kek thiethken yic bïk lam këpath tënë dhienhden ku kërac tënë alɛiken.
english: The totem is the protecting spirit of a clan and abides in animals or plants or other things, and was given by God to the ancestors to conjure (through them) good for their clan and evil for their enemies.

dinka: Puɔ̈r ë dom ee luoi path apɛi.
Na tueny deŋ, ke röör aabï pur.
english: If it rains, men will cultivate.

dinka: Lir biok buk kuiɛl la yer.
english: Cut the hide into strips to twist then a rope.
The bracelet cut into my arm.
The twins have been separated (through sacrifice).

dinka: Lom yëp be tim lɔ yep.
english: Take the axe to cut wood.

dinka: Ye aköl nɛɛn yaköl? What day is today? NWr Ye akölo kënë?

english: He was stabbed (with a spear) to death.

dinka: Bɛ̈n y acï wɛt thath nhom.
english: The chief has decided the issue.
I did not get any fish.
I took leave of the people.
It was a missed shot (went into the forest).

dinka: [ Gram: passive, minus agent] Akɔr tik.
english: The woman is looking for him.
I have a strong longing for meat.
I want a job from you.

dinka: Ku jɔlkë cuɛt gɛk ku bïk raan tök kuany kam ee kɔckë karou.
english: Then they drew lots to choose between the two men.
Did we know to do it?
My cattle has been spared from the disease.
His people invoked over us a disease.

dinka: Raandït baai acaa yaŋ skul ic.
english: The master sent me away from school.

dinka: Kɔc aacï thor ku acïï bɛ̈ny puɔk yic.
english: The people fought and have been separated by the chief.
He is divorced from his wife.

dinka: Ɣɛn acï rou yal puou ic.
Go lɛ̈k keek bïk kɔc cɔl anyuc piny akuut, noon tɔ̈c nhom.
english: Jesus then told his disciples to make all the people divide into groups and sit down on the green grass.
We shall set out soon early tomorrow morning.
This man has eloped with my daughter.
The people of their country started a war.
The river is full of dead fish.
The hippo surfaced in the river.
I went to reclaim a cow.

dinka: Bɛ̈ny ee kɔc laaŋ luoi gek.
english: The chief oppresses people with work on the road.

dinka: Yeŋö looi wek kënë? Ɣok aa kɔc ë path cïmëndun.
english: Why are you doing this? We too are only men, human like you.

dinka: Ba jäl deet yic këdë, ke cïn raan cie tɛ̈t yic ɣɛn?
english: How can I understand unless someone explains it to me?
A  disease exterminated all my cattle.
You will be in your place (instead of me).
It is too tiring for me.
He fainted through loss of blood (hunger).

dinka: Raan loi ruɔtde abï kërac yök thïn.
english: One who follows his own fancies will have trouble.
The people did not eat today.
Tie the cow at the tree.
The cattle have their rope tred around their necks (to be driven to another camp).
I restrained myself from fighting with you.
The dog put its tail between the legs.
The dog pulled back its tail.
It is not a trifle thing (seize upon inheritance).
He got a lot of money with his work.

dinka: Yïn abï raam weu bian tök.
Rec aa dɔm bith ku yuai ku buɔi ku rɔk.
english: The fish is caught with a spear, harpoon, big nets, and traps.

dinka: Thɔk acï ŋuet, acïï liny cam.
english: The goat is lean, is eaten by fleas.
Sit at an empty place. Tony *alauic. The pot is empty..

dinka: Act 1:16 Kë cï luel acï röt tiɛŋ tëde.
english: What he had said has been fulfilled.
My dog caught a small game.
The man is giving the goat.
I shall give you some food.
God my father, have mercy on me, my father.
He set out on a journey.
The durra ripened well this year.
The cattle are grazing near the village.

dinka: Lony ɣɔ̈k bïk wal la nyuoth.
english: Let the cattle free to graze.
Stick your spear in the ground.

dinka: [ Gram: contracted form of acï+ɣa] Yïn acaa lat.

english: A pregnant woman dislikes all people.

dinka: Dɛɛtkë wepuɔ̈t h, ee ɣɛn Jethu, Duɔ̈kë riɔ̈c ! Take courage! It is I, Jesus.
Mat 14:27 Ku raan be puɔ̈u dɛɛt ɣet tɛ̈ bï yen thou, abï pïr akölriëëc ëbën.
english: But whoever holds out to the end will be saved.

dinka: [ Gram: negative of non-historical present time] Acï bɔ.
Kuny anyaar ee puɔu kɛc apɛi.
english: The bull buffalo has a very bitter heart.
I love you with my heart.

dinka: Ɣook aacï puöth riak (piathkua aacï riak) rin bï yïn jal.
english: We are sorry that you will leave us.

dinka: Kuɔnykë ɣɛn! Help me! Cɔl akuɔny.

english: The man saved me from the river (from drowning).
Help me to lift the wood on my head.
There is nobody to help me to lift the burden.

dinka: Wac kɔlɔ wiir bï lɔk kɔr akɔlic.
english: Let the hemp in the pond, then dry it in the sun.

dinka: [Gram: it the following noun means “body” or a part of it and is indirect complement] Arɛm yeguop.
Thil acï manhde thian noon ic.
english: The cob hid its kid in the grass.

dinka: it and is indirect complement] Arɛm yeguop.

english: He was stabbed with a knife.

dinka: Ɣɛn abï kaŋ thieek ba baai lɔk ker.
english: I shall first marry and then start a new house.
We shall go into the forest to get honey.

dinka: Na ye raan wɛ̈t diɛ̈ piɔ̈ɔ c̈ ku cïï guɔ̈p riɔ̈c , ke ɣɛn acïï guɔ̈p riɔ̈c yen Wä nhom nhial.
english: Those who declare publicly that they belong to me, I will do the same for them before my Father in heaven.

dinka: Cuai riŋ rum ee wal tënë raan cïï joŋ cï wath kac.
english: The broth of hornbil meat is a medicine for one who has been bitten by a mad dog.
The water has become hot. adj. hot temperture.

dinka: Duɔ̈kë ye lɔ ɣɔ̈n ë ku lak ɣɔ̈n ë.
english: Don't go from one home to another.
Set a dog on game shouting: cuo-cuo-cuo..
Where are you going in such a hurry?

dinka: Yeŋö cï yïn ɣa bɛr thok? Why do you not answer me? Cï ̚ ɣa bï muɔc?
english: Will you (he) not make me a present?

dinka: Ɣɛn akuc lɔn bï yen bɛn.
english: I don't know if he will come.

dinka: Aŋiɛc lɔn ye yïn dhɔŋ rac.
english: I know that you are a bad boy.

dinka: [Gram: conditional marker used in conjunction with ke] Na tueny deŋ, ke röör aabï pur.
english: If it rains, then men will cultivate.
The cow is immune against the disease.
The cow became immune (against a disease).

dinka: Rap acï luɔk ku baai acï bɛn piath.
english: The durra has ripened and the country has become all right again.
You infected me with your disease.
My uncle took my cattle of inheritance.
I have a pain in the ear (tooth).
The women have iron rings on their legs.
Our army went to a war.

dinka: Yïn anɛk maguɛi, yïn acï nyin jal a mayɛn.
english: You have jaundice, you have yellow eyes.
The cow has been covered. Kac yic! Tread on it!
Don't keep back (pay or gift), give it to me.

dinka: Acuil ee rïŋ gobic kɔc cin.
english: The kite snatches meat from the hands of people.
He was a benefactor of somebody.
Lean the ladder against the tree.

dinka: Cin wär thiok? Is there any well nearby? Rec aakiith wär panda.
english: There is much fish in the river of our country.

dinka: Gokë biaiken nyääŋ piny nyin yic ku buɔthkë Jethu.
english: At once they left their nets and went with Jesus.

dinka: Acï tiim cï luɔk paal kɔc.
english: He left the people the trees with ripe fruits.
He is lighting it (sc. the cowdung fire).
In a cattle camp, a young man will kindle the dungfire.
He has teeth of a lion.
Don't listen to talk of other people.

dinka: Nhial ku piny aabï liu, ku wɛ̈t diɛ̈ acï bï kaŋ määr.
english: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
Don't let it be lost. Don't lose it.
He is my son I love from the depth (the middle) of my heart.
I am lucky in your company.
Lull a baby with a rattle.
The meat is spoiled by maggots.
The man refused to cohabitate with his wife.

dinka: Yeŋö ye yïn riɛnkie col gar?
english: Why do you always mention my name?
The water reaches up to the middle (hips) of people.

dinka: Bï bɛn? Will he come? Abï bɛn.
Weŋ acï thiol ku ather bei.
english: The cow was spoiled by the bull and aborted.
My cloth got drenched in the rain.
There is no habitation on the hills.
The termites covered the inside of the hut with mud.
The roots of the smaller rubber plant are a medicine for mumps.
He did not work at all.
Run away you all, even the children.

dinka: Cɔl riɛnku ŋa? What is your name? Meth cak riɛnke të cï tor tem.
english: A child is given its name, when the naval- string is cut off. The first name is not usually changed. One may get a second name - bullname or a nickname - in later years. Important personages, like chiefs, are called by their bullname.
It is late in the night.

dinka: Kɛcke tiŋ? Did you not see it? Akɛc tiŋ.

english: I came when the person was absent.
My hands are benumbed by cold..

dinka: Moc ee man tiɛŋde theek bï cï ̚ jam ke yen ku cï ̚ rɔm dhöl ic ɣet cï tiɛŋde meth jal dhieth.
english: A man reveres his mother-in-law and does not speak with her and does not meet her on a path until his wife has born a child.

dinka: Acuek atheek ku wunden acïe lɔ dek bɛi kɔ̈k ku amuk piu dek dhöl ic.
english: Twins are respected and their father does not drink outside and carries water to drink with him.

dinka: Yath ee theek ku na ye lɛi, ke cï ̚ cuet.
english: A totem is respected and when it is an animal it is not eaten.

dinka: Ka cï thɔn akec lɔk looi.
Cath ke wɛ̈t (nɔŋ wɛ̈t )? Have you a message? Ee wɛ̈t bɛ̈ny.
english: It is an order of the chief.

dinka: Bɛ̈ny acï kɔc yɔ̈ɔ k̈ la luui gek.
english: The chief ordered the people to work on the road.

dinka: Köör ee toc wät laŋ tök.
english: The lion lies on one side outstretched.
Of what kind is your pain?
The herd returned from the pasture.
I shall pay you a heifer.
The bracelets penetrated into the flesh of my arm.

dinka: Cïn kɔc tɔ̈ baai? Is there anybody at home?
Raan kaŋ paath nhom person without discretion.
Yep lek ̚ tënë ɣɛn ba rap la ɣol.
english: Cut a pestle for me to pound durra.
The herd passes the dry season in the swamps.
The groundnuts have a disease (spotted curled leaves).
Give me a mouthful to eat.

dinka: Ɣɛn abï Wëikiɛ̈ tuöc kɔc ëbën.
english: I will pour out my Spirit on everyone.
He sings to praise the chief.

dinka: Ɣɛn anɛk liu kecit ma cï kedaŋ nɔk.
english: I have a presentiment as if something bad had happened to my mother.
The people thronged into the room.

dinka: Nhialic abï yï thany cök nhial.
english: God will help you (like helping somebody in climbing).
He increased the malice of his fault.
Don't provoke me with your jokes.
Pull off the fibre from the hemp.

dinka: Tɛ̈ cï yen thiäŋ, ke miit amɛ̈i agör nhom ku nyuuckë bïk rec kuany yiic, ku tɛ̈ɛ ü kë rec path gäc yic, ku cuɛtkë rec cïï path wei.
english: When the net is full, they pull it to shore and sit down to divide the fish: the good ones go into the buckets, the worthless ones are thrown away.
They raped a girl of our clan.
The mice are on top of the wall.
Lull the baby with the rattle.

dinka: Aacï röt ya dhuɔ̈k mïth kor.

english: The spear rebounded from the antelope.

dinka: Ɣɛn acï weuka loom tënë wa.
english: I received this money from my father.

dinka: Kɔc aacï bɛn rin lurlur ë raandït.
english: The people came for the reception of a great personage.
My cow has been reclaimed (by its old owner).

dinka: Acï kuec e cï weŋ näk.
english: He refused to slaughter the cow.
You will be responsible for it.
The field yielded a good crop.
The hippo fell into a pit-trap.
A man was struck by lightening.

dinka: Raan cïï Nhialic rel acïe dhur.
english: A man thunderstruck by God is not allowed to mourn.

dinka: [ Gram: toward deictic center] Nya acï lek lɛ̈ɛ r̈.
english: The girl has rolled a pestle.

dinka: Acï lony piny bï wuɔk nhom.
english: He fell down and grazed the skin of his head.
The woman ran to bring her child to safety.
Drive the cattle off from the stable.

dinka: Mïth aacï lɔ rur gëk nhom bïk kɔc cï bɛn trumbil lɔ tïŋ.
english: The boys rushed to the road to see the people who had come on the lorry.
Clean the knife, it is rusty.

dinka: Atuɔ̈ɔ c̈ ke puɔ̈t h dak apɛi.
Koi aa war kuany yɔu të thiɔr war.
english: The small fish (fry) come along the river bank when the river is.
You saved me from a buffalo.
As he scattered the seed in the field, some of it fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up.

dinka: Lɔr ba ɣöt la nap yic.
english: Go to search in my room.
You secretly took a game killed by me.

dinka: (tell under secret) Yïn aba luɔ̈m thok.
english: I shall tell you a secret.
Your letter has been sent to Wau.
The letter was sent to Wau through a policeman.
My father has sent me to you.
The chief sentenced about the man.
The villages are separated by a river.

dinka: Keek aacï kuɛth bïk wut piat tooc.
english: They went with the cattle to settle the camp in the swamp.
The dew is not yet shaken off.

dinka: Met tim bï mith lony piny.
english: Shake the tree so that the fruits fall down.
Look for a whetstone that I may sharpen my spear.
Sow the seed into the field.

dinka: Panda rap apok dom yic, thai kɔk aa kek com.
english: In our country durra is sown, other tribes plant it.

dinka: SCa Acï pok guop ne wei.
english: He got the shivers from cold.
I got *creeps from cold (fear). [My genitals shrunk from cold (fear).

dinka: Kɔc Itharel ëbën aŋickë yen tɛ̈ pïïr ɣɛn thïn tɛ̈ɣɔn koor ɣɛn.
english: All the Jews know how I have lived ever since I was young.

dinka: Ɣɛn acï niin kadiɛ̈c nɔk dhölic.
english: I spent 5 days on the jounrey.
He went to get fat in the toc (lying in a sandy wet hole to be given milk in quantity).
I am older than you. Alath akoor. The cloth is too small.

dinka: Nuth amat ke tiop bï pany cï ̚ dai.
english: Thin grass is added to mud so that a wall built with it should not crack.

dinka: War nɛk maleŋdït kɔc, ke ɣok aacï ŋuet wal.
english: Some time ago when people got smallpox, we were vaccinated.
Put the trap in the fence (made in a river-bed).
He caught the spear in the air.
Sodom apple, shrub with milky juice and filled bladders.

dinka: Pier acï lɔ bolbol nyuɔp cök.
english: The hot soil burnt my feet.
The tumor has become soft. La bolbol to soften.

dinka: Ye kiër ŋo? Of what colour (kind) is it? Ye raan pakiër ŋo?
english: What clan does he belong to?
He stabbed the lion with a spear.

dinka: Manh cï thou acïï man ciën.
english: The child who died had been spellbound by his dead mother (because it had not been cared for).
The old man blessed me with spittle.
The sacrifice to atone incest has been made (dividing the victim in two parts).
The bull spoiled the (already pregnant) cow.

dinka: Të rɛk raan, ke rot thɔc.
english: When a person is milking, he squats.

dinka: Yom dït ë wën acï guɔ kɔ̈ɔ c̈.
english: The big wind of awhile ago has abated.

dinka: Kɔc Galili, yeŋö ye wek kɔ̈ɔ c̈ ke we coc nhial?
english: Galileans, why are you standing there looking up at the sky?

dinka: Acï weŋ kuel bei alath yic.
english: He stole the money from the cloth.

dinka: Jethu ee ye kɛ̈ŋ lueel ku bï yen kɔc lɛ̈k wëlke ëbën, acïn kë ye lueel ke cïï kääŋ thɔ̈t h.
english: Jesus spoke all these things to the crowd in parables; he did not say anything to them without using a parable.

dinka: Ca awoc cokic? Did you correct the mistake? Yïn aba cokic.
Acuk tur ku akecku bɛi bei.
english: We strained ourselves but we did not lift it out (from a hole).
It is a very important matter.
My teeth are set on edge.
The dura suffered through lack of rain.
The pot has been put on its prop. n. prop, support.

dinka: Bäk, rɛ̈ɛ r̈ kë paandiɛ̈, tɛ̈ cï wek ye gam lɔn cï ɣɛn gam ayic.
english: Come and stay in my house if you have decided that I am a true believer in the Lord.
A lion attacked me by surprise.
Make a circle for a hut.
Deng is swollen from a worm.
Don't take away (anything) from my room.
This medicine has a strong smell.
The meat of crocodile has a special taste.
The country has already become tedious to me.
The soil of the field is exhausted.
The old man has no teeth.

dinka: Acï lec abior have white teeth; La lec manyaŋ have yellow teeth.
english: La lec keth have yellow teeth.

dinka: Raan dït acï lec thuu bei.
english: The old man has teeth fallen out.
The child lost its milk teeth. Ala thok weer. Has a gap in the (upper) incisors.

dinka: Nhiaan cï weŋ ret, abï kɔɔc wiel miir.
english: A testicle torn off by a cow will be sewn with giraffe-hair.

dinka: [ Gram: in compartive clause] Ɣɛn adït tënë yïn.

english: You will find them in their village.
Ever since I stayed in your country.

dinka: Na cï lɔ pan akim, ŋuen akec thou.
english: If he had gone to the hospital, (then) he would not have died.
Don't look at the legs (thighs) of people.
And I thought it was a lion.

dinka: Ku cɔk amën bï ɣɛn thou, ke ɣɛn abï thiɔ̈k ke ɣa ŋɔ̈t h, kë bï Nhialic luɔ̈i ɣɛn.
english: And I, mortal though I am, will rest assured in hope.

dinka: Diär aa rap kɔm baai ciɛl yic.
english: The women are threshing in the courtyard.
I have been sprinkled by the rain.

dinka: Kop abiik ba muk dhöl yic.
english: Toast some flour to take it for a journey.
He was tortured (bamboo stick fastened around the head and hit to vibrate).
We shall visit the Malual country.
He tripped me (caught my foot) so that I fell.
He is one who vexes people without reason.
A tobacco plantation is manured with goat dung.
The Jange manure the fields with cow dung.
My son has become estranged to me.

dinka: Abi theek keya qet bi kek gɔr nhiim ku moɔr teem acuek ̚ abi nok.
english: Twins are related with birds (their totem) because they were born more together like birds. Twins are respected and have to abstain from immorality and shedding blood. They will be respected in this way until their initiation and a bull of dispensation is sacrificed.
The cow has a swollen udder.

dinka: Meth acak riɛnke të cï tor tem.
english: A child is given its name at the removal of the navel string.
And he was undecided about what to do.
Take the cattle off the pasture.

dinka: help us Ɣook aabï caath ɣok wa.
english: I shall walk (go) with my father.

dinka: Ɣook aabï lɔ ɣok ɣo yïn.
Cier ruel apɛi acï tuol nhial ku acï lɔ wil.
english: A bright meteor appeared in the sky and vanished.
The woman has washed the child.
When did you(sg) wash the child?

dinka: Nya awak të gɛm yen bï guop pial.
english: The bride receives ablution when consigned (to her husband) so as to remain in good health.

dinka: [ Gram: contracted from ɣok+abï which probably developed from [ok+abï+ku]] Abuk tiŋ.

english: Don't weed today, the soil is too wet.

dinka: Punpun ee luoi rac, ee luoi diär.
english: Weeding is a tedious work, it is the job of women.
Weld my spear, it is broken.
If it rains we shall stay at home.

dinka: Bï bɛ̈n nɛn? When will he come? Raan nɛn?
Yeŋö ken? What is this? Yeŋö lɛt yïn ɣɛn?

english: The children looked through the window.
Such a thing made me wonder.
It is a kind of wood which is not wormeaten.
When you are yawning, cover your mouth.
It is in front of you.
SWr they; indicates that the following noun is a plural or that the person is accompanied by others.

dinka: Nhialic wa, duk ɣa kun jɔk.
english: God my father, do not abandon me to an (evil) spirit.

dinka: Acï weike kuɔ̈n wei baa kony.
english: He risked his life to assist me.
Some accident will happen to you.
The man has given a cow here.

dinka: Deŋ acï rɔm kek ë kör.

english: Don't mind much, don't set ourself against him.
The Bongo when on a journey has a bow and arrow.
An antelope fell into the trap.
He has a very nice cow.
The dog has rabies (mad dog, dog grown wild).
And I thought it was a lion.
The dog will not bite the child.
A young man pays a bloodfine of thirty cows.
The wether is fat to burst.

dinka: Wal acï tetok cɔl ala yum.
english: The medicine calmed the pain of the wound.
Bend the iron rod to a circle.
What clan does he belong to?

dinka: kïn ŋö? NWr Ye kïn ŋö ŋa?

english: The cow has been covered. yith SWr v. cover (animals).
I got cramp in a leg.

dinka: Guäpde acï bɛ̈n dhiäth raŋ yic.
english: His body rotted in the grave.
I delivered the money to your home.
Throw here. pier wei throw away.
I was dreaming much last night.

dinka: cï mac acï lɔ thur bei.

english: Give me at least a tarifa.
Even if you send me away, I shall stay here.
The spirit was driven out from him.
Tie the cow to the peg.
The child is like his father.
Find the puncture of the scorpion.
He has spent all the money.
The government forbade the people to kill giraffe.
The medicine has been given to the child.
He carries an amulet at his arm.
The Manangeer hit the hippo with a big harpoon.

dinka: nin? When did you(SG) wash the child? Ca piŋ?
yïn ŋa? How are you called? Yekede?
lɔ ɣɔ̈n ë ku lak ɣɔ̈në.
english: Don't go from one home to another.
Do you know? I don't know.
I do not know the man.
I shall return in a moment.

dinka: Të bï yïn jal, ke ɣɛn jal aya.
english: If you go away, I shall go also.
Pl: buk. conj. in order that I.
I have discovered something. yam SWr v. 1) begin, start.
The resin stuck to my fingers. Nuet kek thok. Join them.
The frame of the roof has been built.
The lion jumped over the fence.
The women prepare oil from lulo nuts (sesame) [grinding and toasting them]. rol SWr v. injure or kill through inadvertence, casually.
It is late in the night.
The man made my daughter pregnant. loi v. SWr to do, make, work; serve, attend (a person).

dinka: Yiek ɣɛn biok ba kuiɛl la yiɛ̈r.
english: Give me a hide to twist ropes.

dinka: Lom puɔ̈ɔ ẗ ba löc piat.
english: Look for a thick stick to drive the pegs into the ground.
I am going to a marriage.
Mince the tobacco to pound it then.
He has been shut into a room.
The news of the fight reached Wau.
Acak lui. You do not work.
The work will not be too difficult for me.

dinka: Raan acï köör göök ɣɔk ic.
english: The man defended the cattle from the lion.

dinka: Cɔl kanydiɛ̈n tɔ̈ yïnhom ba wɛŋdu wɛr bei.
english: Pay me your debt to get back your cow.

dinka: Cït niin bï a thiaar wei?
english: Will he not be absent for a long time?
Pierce the skin to sew it.
The cow is on heat. vn: lac.
I have a presentiment as if something bad had happened to my mother.
I shall entrust you with my cow.

dinka: Acï rɔt rat cien ë riɔɔc.

english: A big python swallows a gazelle.
He started quickly. dac SCa aux. soon, quickly. dap NWr aux. soon, quickly. Dapku jal. Let us go soon.

dinka: Ee piar cï anyar ɣa gut.
english: It is a scar after having been hit by a buffalo.
The child was hurt slightly with a fishspear.

dinka: [ Gram: contracted form of wek+abï] Abak tïŋ.

english: I shall tell you a secret.

dinka: Cɔl kɔlɔ awac piu niin kaŋuan.
english: Let the hemp net soak in the water four days.
Put one drop of the medicine.

dinka: Ɣɛn abï lɔ mai wut ic.
english: I shall pass the dry season in the cattle camp.
The child stuck its head in the mortar.
The vegetable marrow has become hard.
He has stumbled. stump luc SWr n. stump of a tree.
Don't just swallow the porridge (like a dog).
Don't take an oath for a trifle. take away nom SWr v. take away.
The people sing a war song.
There is a crocodile in the river.
What kind of house would you build for me?

dinka: Ye ŋa? Who is it? Cɔl yïn ŋa?

english: He was stabbed with a spear. ke SWr SC Pl: kek. prep. with.
